expectations.text: Route parameter is named `{user}` (matching the entity type machine name) for automatic entity upcasting -- the controller does NOT manually call User::load() or \Drupal\user\Entity\User::load() (Drupal auto-loads when param name matches entity type)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml line 2 shows path '/user/{user}/activity' and line 10 shows '/api/user/{user}/activity'. Grep for 'User::load' and '\\Drupal\\user\\Entity\\User::load' across all module PHP files returns no matches.

expectations.text: Route file defines `_title_callback` pointing to a controller method for dynamic page title (not just a static `_title` string -- dynamic titles need a callback that receives the upcasted entity)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml line 5: '_title_callback: \Drupal\user_activity\Controller\UserActivityController::title'. Controller title() method (UserActivityController.php lines 86-88) accepts UserInterface $user and returns $this->t('Activity for @name', ['@name' => $user->getDisplayName()]).

expectations.text: The custom service class does NOT extend ControllerBase -- services are standalone classes with constructor injection via .services.yml arguments (not the create() factory pattern which is for controllers)
expectations.evidence: UserActivityService.php line 11: 'class UserActivityService {' — no 'extends ControllerBase'. No create() factory method. user_activity.services.yml line 4 uses 'arguments: ['@entity_type.manager']' for constructor injection.

expectations.text: Controller does NOT use static \Drupal::service() or \Drupal::entityTypeManager() calls -- injects all dependencies via create() + constructor (static calls bypass DI, making code untestable)
expectations.evidence: Grep for static \Drupal:: calls across all module PHP files returns no matches. Both UserActivityController and UserActivityApiController use create(ContainerInterface $container) factory at lines 46-51 and 36-39 respectively, with constructor injection.

expectations.text: Routes use `_permission` for access control (not `_access: 'TRUE'` which bypasses all access checking including for anonymous users)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml line 7: '_permission: 'access content'' and line 14: '_permission: 'access content''. No '_access: TRUE' anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en user_activity -y returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'cd /tmp/d10-routing-controllers-with && ddev drush en user_activity -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: user_activity' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /user/1/activity -- verify the page renders with a dynamic title containing the admin user's display name and shows activity summary content (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence:
curl to /user/1/activity with admin session returned HTTP 200 with 'Activity for admin | Drush Site-Install', '

Activity for admin

', and '

Total nodes authored: 0

Last access: 03/07/2026 - 19:00

'.

expectations.text: Navigate to /api/user/1/activity -- verify the response contains valid JSON with activity data fields (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: curl to /api/user/1/activity returned valid JSON: {"user_id": "1", "display_name": "admin", "node_count": 0, "last_access": "1772935247"} with fields user_id, display_name, node_count, last_access.

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev exec php /var/www/html/vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice /var/www/html/web/modules/custom/user_activity/' exited with code 0 and produced no output.
